2605.24686.title: Emotional intelligence in large language models is fragmented across perception, cognition, and interaction
2605.24686.paper_summary: As large language models (LLMs) are increasingly integrated into emotionally sensitive domains, the structural integrity of their emotional intelligence (EI) becomes a critical frontier for safety and alignment. Current benchmarks often conflate superficial politeness with deep affective reasoning, failing to distinguish between perceptual accuracy and interactive efficacy. Here, we introduce FACET (Functional Affective Competence and Empathy Test), a psychometrically grounded framework comprising 480 expert-crafted items. Unlike previous metrics, FACET is theoretically anchored in the Mayer-Salovey-Caruso four-branch ability model, operationalizing EI through perception, facilitation, understanding, and management of emotions. Through an evaluation of nine frontier models (including GPT-5, Claude-Sonnet-4), we demonstrate that emotional intelligence is not a monolithic capability but is fragmented across cognitive and interactive dimensions. While frontier models demonstrate robust proficiency in objective emotion recognition and social reasoning, this does not consistently translate to interactive success. We categorize these discrepancies into three distinct performance profiles: cognitive-dominant, interactive-dominant, and context-dependent. These typologies indicate that emotional skills do not scale uniformly with general intelligence or model size; rather, they are shaped by specific alignment paradigms. Notably, we identify hidden emotion recognition as a universal performance bottleneck across all architectures. Our results suggest that current RLHF processes may optimize for "stochastic empathy", a statistical mimicry of emotional syntax, at the expense of integrated affective reasoning. These findings challenge the assumption of linear emotional scaling and provide a rigorous roadmap for developing socially aware agents capable of genuine clinical resonance.
2605.24686.paper_summary_zh: 隨著大型語言模型（LLMs）越來越多地融入情感敏感的領域，它們的情感智慧（EI）的結構完整性成為安全性和一致性的一個重要前沿。當前的基準常常將表面的禮貌與深層的情感推理混為一談，未能區分感知準確性和互動效能。在此，我們介紹FACET（功能性情感能力與同理心測試），這是一個基於心理測量學的框架，包含480個專家設計的項目。與以往的指標不同，FACET在理論上基於Mayer-Salovey-Caruso四分支能力模型，通過對情感的感知、促進、理解和管理來操作化EI。通過對九個前沿模型（包括GPT-5、Claude-Sonnet-4）的評估，我們證明情感智慧並不是一種單一的能力，而是在認知和互動維度上是碎片化的。雖然前沿模型在客觀情感識別和社會推理方面展現出強大的能力，但這並不總是能夠轉化為互動的成功。我們將這些差異歸類為三種不同的表現型態：認知主導型、互動主導型和情境依賴型。這些類型表明，情感技能並不會隨著一般智力或模型大小的增加而均勻擴展；相反，它們受到特定一致性範式的影響。值得注意的是，我們發現隱性情感識別是所有架構中的一個普遍性能瓶頸。我們的結果表明，當前的RLHF過程可能優化了“隨機同理心”，這是一種情感語法的統計模仿，卻以整合的情感推理為代價。這些發現挑戰了線性情感擴展的假設，並為開發能夠真實臨床共鳴的社會意識代理提供了一個嚴謹的路線圖。
2605.24686.author: Minghao Lv et.al.
2605.24686.authors: Minghao Lv, Lu Chen, Enchang Zhang, Anji Zhou, Xiaoran Xue, Hanyi Zhang, Fenghua Tang, Zhuo Rachel Han, Mengyue Wu
